channel.title: Comments for page "Portraits Of Your Father"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Portraits Of Your Father"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: happy birthday

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw fuck me man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy fucking shit. Just… holy shit. This is visceral and amazing and made me cry and it deserves so much more praise. +1 through actual real life tears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. I'm not sure what I could say that wasn't already said, but this is definitely the best tale I've ever read on this site. How it's only at 100 (only, I say), I have no idea. I was a little cautious at the sheer size of it, but i was hooked from only a few sentences in.

Dad takes you to see the butterflies that call you things like “PROGENY” and “CHILD” with their wings even though you’ve told them your name before.

I didn't expect this to hit as hard as it did. You also turned something silly like Duke Til Dawn into a almost tearful moment.

You’re painting a portrait of something old and something new, and everything inbetween.

Just like your father.

I was almost bawling by the end of this. Your skill as a writer is something I aspire to match, though I'm not sure I'll ever get there.

+1. If I could give you a hundred more, I would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Congratulations, you made me cry at 10:30 on a Wednesday night.

This feels too good for some dumpy backwater fanfic site, legitimately one of the best things I’ve ever read, SCP or otherwise.

Take your +1 and I wish I could give a thousand more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had to fight extremely hard to hold back tears. I didn't want my Mom and Dad know that I shed tears over SCP stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instant +1, someone please get me some tissues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god. This is beautiful I'm actually crying because of this. +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never cry but here we are.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a few years late to the party, but Jesus. This is like… unbelievably visceral. The writing is fantastic and the emotional impact was like a physical punch to the gut. I started sobbing and was so overcome that I almost threw up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exceptionally beautiful and heart-wrenching. This felt very personal and very real, even without any comparable events in my life to relate it to. There's many works on this site that I love simply for the creativity of the ideas contained therein, but the literary quality of the writing here is really stand-out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Burst into tears after reading.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was absolutely fantastic and moving. I'm not very familiar with any of the foundation characters, as I normally just read the scp articles, but James and draven are now tall is my favorite characters ever haha. What a gripping story. But I will definitely not be reading pet sematary. I have a toddler and I teared up just reading that one excerpt. Oof.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is speaking of J.M. Talloran, the man who defeated 3999, right? From the perspective of his boyfriend? Regarding the life he lived before the events of 3999? Just making sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great honor for me to translate this article into Chinese. I have cried for several times when reading it,deciding to apply for an SCPwiki account to show my respect to you.Thank you for this perfect sense of visual perception.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Holy guacamole on macaroni. That was an absolute rollercoaster.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did you do this to my emotions.

I'll be back to make an audio adaptation of this some day; I don't trust myself to play both parts and I don't currently have an acting partner I trust enough to play the emotion. But thank you for writing something I want to portray so thoroughly because the pool is THAT deep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to Draven's creator, Rights isn't actually his mother. I had assumed so too, but I was too hasty to add that tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I might cry. This is a tour de force in dialogue, pacing, and character, and a fantastic reimagining of the man who rode 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome. Duke till dawn being a manic fantasy of someone with bipolar disorder sounds very right. Of all the ends to Kondraki, this is the one that most feels like it would have happened if the Foundation existed in the real world. Not sure how I feel about Dr Rights being the mother, or about James not knowing she's a doctor despite being a researcher and the apparent legendary status of many of the rest of the senior staff. Still, a hearty +1 for you.

Also:

I think he’s touching on through it okay.

should be toughing? I'm not sure or I'd have fixed it myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember when you announced this one on tumblr and I've meant to read it, thankfully somebody brought it up in 19 today or it might've slipped my mind.

This hits…..really close to home for me. Really, really, really close to home, to the point that I almost couldn't finish it because it was making me remember things I didn't want to. But in the end I think that's precisely why I enjoyed it so much, even if it physically hurt to read. I could write probably an entire essay about why, but a review's not really the place for that.

Upvoted pretty much immediately because holy shit.

item.title: Re: New Hell Tale Posted
item.content:encoded:
Everything on the site is a fanfic, I don't know what you mean.

This is some gripping shit. I'm onto section 5 now — so far I think you have some lines that can better be left implied (e.g. Draven's line in 1 "When are the good ones?" doesn't need to be explicitly described as helpness) but I am digging your paragraph-long sentences so hard. I am digging the style so hard. If present tense was a person I'd fuck it. I only have one upvote but this deserves several more. +1 ^.^

edit: you have a new fan it's me and i love you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I had the time to go back and give this a really thorough copy-edit, because it could seriously use one.

But I'm really glad that I had the time to read this, because it is very good indeed. Turning Kondraki into a character that feels real without rewriting the mythos entirely is quite an achievement. This is keenly observed.

I think the saddest thing is realising that Draven's behaviour, especially towards the very end of the tale, shows hints of the same mental illness that affected his father. A portrait indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are one of my favorite writers on this site. Please post more fanfic here, omg.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm speechless. This is beautiful. +1 and praise. Holy shit.

There's some grammatical/spelling errors here and there, but because the entire thing is stream-of-consciousness, I'm not sure whether these are intentional:

+There's a lot of these because this tale has a lot of words
-Close

1

immediately lurches towards him hands drunkenly grasping for him

him, hands

He rarely got would get his wish.

Not sure what happened here

whatever he says to you he doesn’t mean it and I’m sorry and starts on the other shoe

Cursive goes on for longer than I'd expect

Draven gets up from the bed and moved up to the front of the bed.

Mixed past and present tense
2

when stagnant blood from his father’s head runs over his arms and plaster’s his shirt to his body

Plasters

and he think’s that was what sent him to the phone

Thinks
4

you take it out and read it allowed to him

Aloud? The story is being told from the perspective of a child.
6

different frames then he had when he was older.

Than

a couple years past it’s due date,

Its

you didn’t think it would fucking matter, or that nobody would care!”

Double negation; anybody (dialogue; could be intentional)

the polish countryside

Capitalisation (more instances of the same "error" omitted)

Draven it sitting up

Is (typo?)

James wish he could stop this

Wishes?

05 will get back to us shortly of their appointment for the position,

Of —> about (I assume)

any paperwork submitted after with and elucid or lower level of urgency

"Euclid" and unsure how to make this sentence grammatically correct.
8

It becomes clear when as they clean that his dad hadn’t bothered to come home for quite a while longer than they thought.

"When" is redundant
9

He runs faster from you then he does from Duke

Than

you manage to get him away from the stairwell and chase him down an adjacent hallway instead, and when his squad commander squeaks over his radio

2nd person storytelling switches to 3rd person storytelling

poorly-thought-out

Poorly thought-out

The fact that your dad fought back was enough by regulation to backup your choice to bring him down,

Back up
10

He lets him to the talking

To —> do
11

eight hours before they had you here

Before—>ago/earlier
12

and it's these queues that send Draven from the bedroom door to the garage

Queues—>cues

Your father loved you and was always scared to loose you.

Lose

it’s easier to read and forget then cry and remember.

Then—>than (to)
15

petal to the metal

Pedal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, when's your book coming out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why must you hurt me in this way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the most beautiful, deeply affecting things I've read on the site. The weapons-grade heaviness of the subject matter is treated with such care and insight that it's hard to remember it's not real. It feels less like a regular tale and more like an accomplishment, and a perfect example of how this site has evolved from "creepypasta with rules" into so much more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may not be in the same continuity as Major Tom but it sure as hell has the same gut-wrenching effect as it. Jesus Christ.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed! Thanks.

EDIT: Also I'm glad you took the strike throughs as an artistic thing, because that was on account of Google Docs not translating into wikidot well, lol. Has been fixed also.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really loved this, the use of strike throughs to indicate dialogue he wanted to say but couldn't say was an inspired choice. Btw, I think you meant 105 in Iris' section? You wrote 166.

item.title: New Hell Tale Posted
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this sure is a wild time.

Some things and stuff:

1. Draven actually…isn't my character? This is the first time he's been put on-site, but he actually belongs to V1als on tumblr. James is mine. They're dorks. (EDIT as of November 15th, 2017): James is actually James Talloran from SCP-3999. It's canon now, as in, it's in the actual 3999 article. We made it big lads

2. Parts of this tale are meta. What can you do.

3. This is so long, like yikes.

4. Just like Major Tom, I never meant for this to get this long and go on the site. This started as a fanfic. Unlike Major Tom, this took me probably a week of binge writing to finish as opposed to eight months.

5. This doesn't take place in the Major Tom universe. That's not to say that I'm not working on a ton of new stuff to go in the Major Tom universe, though.

6. I'm…not sure how happy I am with this, but it's…okay.

7. Stephen King's Pet Sematary is amazing and you should read it.
